mnemonics: IN-PLACE REVERSAL: 'No New Nodes Needed' - think of flipping a row of books on a shelf without buying new ones, just rearranging the links.
keyPoints: Iterate through the list with three pointers: previous, current, and next
Update the next pointer of the current node to point to the previous node
The head of the original list becomes the tail of the reversed list
commonMistakes: Losing reference to the next node before updating pointers, causing a broken list
Forgetting to update the head pointer to point to the new first node after reversal
Not handling edge cases like empty list or single-node list correctly
